sun-wukong-subtitles_file0.show: movies Alexis Gideon Video Musics II Sun Wu Kong (with subtitles, lower quality tho)
sun-wukong-subtitles_file0.desc:
"Video Musics II: Sun Wu-Kong" is a one hour multimedia video opera based on the 16th century Chinese novel "The Journey to the West". The project combines line drawing and watercolor animation as well as stop motion and claymation.



It has musical contributions from Rachel Blumberg (M Ward, Arch Cape), Cory Gray (Norfolk & Western, Carcrashlander), Shelley Short (Hush) as well as visual contributions from Cynthia Star (Coraline, Moral Orel, Robot Chicken), Becca Taylor (Punk Planet, Arthur) and Ezra Claytan Daniels (Black Violet, The Changers).



The DVD/Vinyl release is on Africantape(EU) / Slowtrain(US) scheduled for fall 2010.

PXLTHIS_03_file1.desc: The Toy Camera Film Festival (Fisher Price PXL 2000 - Pixelvision) celebrated its 20th year in 2010 with an eclectic program of short films from award-winning experimental filmmakers, children, homeless and Hollywood professionals. http://www.laughtears.com/PXL-THIS-20.html features Pixelvision films made with the Fisher-Price PXL-2000 camcorder. PXL THIS, the second oldest film festival in LA, celebrates visionary moving image artists from 4-years-olds to professionals. More info: Gerry Fialka pfsuzy@aol.com 310-306-7330 Bio - http://www.laughtears.com/bio.html and http://www.laughtears.com/ Deadline for entry every year is Oct 22, send dvd to PXL THIS 2427 1/2 Glyndon Av, Venice CA 90291, no entry fee For PXL THIS websites visit: http://pxl2000.blogspot.com/ and http://sites.google.com/site/pxlthis/ and http://www.youtube.com/user/pxlthis

word_sword_file1.desc:
Word Sword is a 2006 British Christian drama film directed by Philip Whitfield. The film revolves around a group of people whose lives are destroyed by drugs and other vices.

While this film resembles some kind of experimental video art project made for public access television (or a Tim and Eric sketch), it was apparently meant to be a serious morality play.
